output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford incurred capital expenditures for two types of improvements related to the factory. The first expenditure of $1,750,000 for fencing, streetlights, and walkways represents depreciable site improvements that enhance the utility of the property. The second expenditure of $2,360,000 for permanent landscaping installations represents land improvements that become part of the property. Under IAS 16, both are capitalized as property, plant, and equipment. The site improvements are depreciable over their useful lives, while the permanent landscaping treatment depends on whether it becomes part of the land (non-depreciable) or represents separate improvements (depreciable). Given the description "permanent landscaping installations," these are treated as land improvements. Cash was used for the first payment and cheque for the second.
